name: Gesha

migrationPath.notes: Introduced for leaf rust resistance research
Acquired designation T2722; largely overlooked for decades
Planted; rediscovered in 2004 by the Peterson family
Broke specialty auction records at $20+/lb; set off global demand
Planted across Latin America chasing the Panama profile

notableProducers: Neto (La Margarita, Ocobamba, Peru — Yellow Gesha, unconfirmed)
description: Gesha originated from coffee forests in the Kaffa region of Ethiopia, near the village of Gesha. After traveling through Tanzania and Costa Rica (where it sat unnoticed for decades as T2722), it was planted in Panama in the 1960s and rediscovered in 2004. The Peterson family entered it into Panama's Best of Panama competition in 2005, where it fetched record-breaking prices and ignited global demand. It has since been planted across Latin America — though genetic confirmation varies by lot. Some coffees sold as 'Gesha' have no Gesha genetics at all.
